summary: The subject, a technology development manager, engaged in unauthorized exfiltration of confidential company data. Evidence includes communication with an external conspirator (spy.conspirator@nist.gov), use of personal email (iaman.informant.personal@gmail.com) for data transfer, and the installation of anti-forensic tools (CCleaner, Eraser) to conceal activity. The subject also performed unauthorized access to secured network drives and exhibited anomalous logon patterns. EVIDENCE COVERAGE MATRIX: [Filesystem: YES] [Registry: NO] [EventLogs: YES] [NetworkLogs: YES]
null_hypothesis: The subject was attempting to bypass DLP controls for personal convenience rather than malicious exfiltration, and the anti-forensic tools were used to hide personal browsing history rather than corporate data destruction.

artifacts.name: External Conspirator Email
artifacts.description: External email address communicating with the subject regarding confidential data.
artifacts.recommended_validation: Cross-reference with HR records and external threat intelligence.

artifacts.name: Personal Exfiltration Account
artifacts.description: Personal Gmail account used to exfiltrate corporate data.
artifacts.recommended_validation: Audit DLP logs for attachments sent from this account.

artifacts.name: Anti-Forensic Tools
artifacts.description: Installation of tools used to wipe evidence after exfiltration.
artifacts.recommended_validation: Review installation logs and file system timestamps.

artifacts.name: Anomalous Network Access
artifacts.description: Access to secured network drive followed by immediate disconnection.
artifacts.recommended_validation: Review firewall and VPN logs for session duration.
